aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

surface_forms: Marine Corps is always two up and one back
description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

entity: United States Naval Reserve Officers Training Corps
description: military officer training program for the United States Navy and United States Marine Corps
description_wiki: The Naval Reserve Officer Training Corps (NROTC) program is a college-based, commissioned officer training program of the United States Navy and the United States Marine Corps.

description_wiki: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) is a private research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States.

description_wiki: Quantico (; formerly Potomac) is a town in southeastern Prince William County, Virginia, United States.

description_wiki: Yokosuka (横須賀市, Yokosuka-shi; pronounced [jokosɯ̥ka]) is a city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan.

description: metropolitan city of South Korea
description_wiki: Busan (Korean: 부산; pronounced [pusan]), officially Busan Metropolitan City, is the second most populous city in South Korea, after Seoul; it has a population of over 3.3 million as of 2024.

description: infantry battalion of the United States Marine Corps
description_wiki: 1st Battalion, 5th Marines (1/5) is an infantry battalion in the United States Marine Corps based out of Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, California consisting of approximately 800 Marines and sailors.

aliases: Veterans History Project of the Library of Congress American Folklife Center
Veterans History Project, Library of Congress
description: collection of firsthand remembrances of U.S. wartime veterans
description_wiki: The Veterans History Project of the Library of Congress American Folklife Center (commonly known as the Veterans History Project) was created by the United States Congress in 2000 to collect and preserve the firsthand remembrances of U.S. wartime veterans.

description: country in West Asia
